documents.title: Colorado State University-Fort Collins public AI hub or guidance
Colorado State University-Fort Collins AI policy/guidance source

ai_strategy.summary: Colorado State University-Fort Collins's public AI posture is visible through governance guidance, teaching resources, research activity, and enterprise/tool support. The evidence points to a pragmatic R1 approach: encourage AI literacy and research while constraining sensitive data and academic-integrity risks.

key_provisions: Use AI in ways consistent with academic integrity and existing university policy.
Protect sensitive, confidential, student, research, or regulated data when using AI tools.
Faculty or academic units retain responsibility for clear course-level expectations where applicable.
Human review and accountability remain necessary for AI-assisted work.
ai_policy.summary: Colorado State University-Fort Collins has public AI guidance or policy-adjacent materials. The cited guidance addresses responsible use, data protection, ethics, and course or work-context expectations.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI framework and AI at CSU hub AI governance and guidance
initiatives.description: Colorado State University-Fort Collins publishes AI guidance or governance resources that frame responsible use, academic integrity, privacy, and institutional oversight. The cited materials make AI a university-level issue rather than only a classroom technology topic.
initiatives.significance: This is a material public signal of how an R1 university is translating AI from discussion into institutional practice.

initiatives.title: AUCC Foundations Project writing-assessment redesign
initiatives.description: CSU’s AUCC 3B-3D Foundations Project recruits faculty to redesign writing assignments in the age of GenAI, co-create and test an assessment rubric in spring 2025, collect student work samples, and disaggregate assessment results across demographic groups.
initiatives.significance: This directly links AI assessment redesign with subgroup-aware learning-outcome assessment.

regulatory_context.summary: Colorado public AI activity is relevant context; this profile treats campus policy and institutional evidence as the primary governance layer.

narrative_summary: Colorado State University-Fort Collins now has clearer source-backed evidence for enterprise AI tools and AI-resilient assessment. CSU lists CSU-GPT, Microsoft Copilot Chat, and Microsoft Teams Premium as approved tools for sensitive data, with CSU-GPT available to every student, faculty, and staff member inside a secure Microsoft Azure environment. The AUCC Foundations Project adds a rare public assessment-redesign signal: faculty redesign writing assignments around GenAI, co-create rubrics, review student work, and plan demographic disaggregation. Public evidence still does not identify a comprehensive AI budget, exact user counts, or a durable GPU count for campus research computing.
